data.description: $COIN - ANALYST DOUBLE DOWNGRADES COINBASE TO SELL Monness, Crespi, Hardt downgraded Coinbase from Buy to Sell, citing prolonged crypto market weakness. The firm cut revenue, EBITDA, and EPS forecasts, now expecting softness through 2026 and 2027. While long-term bullish on

data.title: Palantir Tech (PLTR) stock rises as it receives DISA authorization for PFCS Forward, broadening its deployment flexibility.
data.description: Palantir Technologies Inc. announced that it obtained authorization from the Defense Information Systems Agency for its Federal Cloud Service (PFCS) Forward, expanding its existing Impact Level 5 and 6 Provisional Authorizations to include on-premises and edge deployments. This move allows for a more flexible deployment of its technology stack across various environments, reducing the time required for customers to achieve Authorization to Operate (ATO). The authorization enables the U.S. Government to deploy multivendor architectures efficiently for mission-critical workloads, enhancing operational resilience and survivability. Palantir, an AI and analytics company, aims to drive efficiency through data utilization.

data.title: Xerox distributes pro rata warrants to shareholders on Feb 12, 2026, with a record date of Feb 9, 2026, receiving one warrant per share.
data.description: Xerox Holdings Corporation disclosed the distribution of Warrants to purchase shares of Xerox common stock (the “Warrants”) to eligible securityholders on February 12, 2026. This aligns with the pro rata warrant distribution plan. Shareholders recorded on February 9, 2026 (the “Record Date”) obtained one (1) Warrant for each share owned. Xerox (NASDAQ: XRX) made this announcement from Norwalk, Conn.
Secured overnight financing rate: 3.65% February 11th vs 3.65% February 10th

data.title: Melco Resorts and Enter surpassed Q4 earnings estimates by 54.44% with EPS of $0.139. Revenue rose by $102 million year-on-year.
data.description: Melco Resorts and Enter (NASDAQ: MLCO) released its Q4 earnings report on Thursday, February 12, 2026 at 07:56 AM. The company surpassed expected earnings with an EPS of $0.139, exceeding estimates of $0.09 by 54.44%. Revenue also saw an increase of $102.00 million compared to the previous year. This positive financial performance highlights the company's continued growth and success in the market. For more detailed information, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Conduent's Q4 2025 revenue: $770M, Pre-tax Income: $(28)M; Full year 2025 revenue: $3,042M, Pre-tax Income: $(160)M. CEO outlined priorities.
data.description: Conduent Incorporated reported their Q4 and Full Year 2025 financial results with revenue of $770 million in Q4 and $3,042 million for the entire year. The pre-tax income was a loss of $28 million in Q4 and a loss of $160 million for the year. Adjusted EBITDA margin was 6.5% in Q4 and 5.4% for the year. New Business Signings ACV was $152 million in Q4 and $517 million for the year. CEO stated mixed execution with improving revenue trends in Government and Transportation segments. The priorities for the company include accelerating execution, enforcing financial discipline, reducing cost structure, optimizing the portfolio, converting pipeline into growth, and simplifying the organizational structure. Conduent had approximately $243 million in cash balance at the end of 2025. Financial results mentioned are subject to risks, uncertainties, and outside influences as specified in the forward-looking statements section. Conduent aims to deliver digital business solutions spanning commercial, government, and transportation sectors leveraging advanced technologies.

data.title: IPG Photonics had a 17% revenue increase in Q4 2025, reaching $274.5M. Bookings growth reflects higher revenue demand. Gross margin was impacted negatively. The company's net income rose to $13.3M. Adjusted EBITDA was $41.2M. Plans for a $100M share repurchase program were announced. Revenue outlook for Q1 2026 is $235M-$265M, with adjusted EPS expected to be $0.10-$0.40.
data.description: IPG Photonics Corporation reported financial results for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025. Revenue was $274.5 million, a 17% increase year-over-year, with gross margin at 36.1%. Operating income was $3.3 million, a 76% decrease from the previous year. Net income increased by 71% to $13.3 million, with earnings per diluted share at $0.31, a 72% increase. Adjusted EBITDA was $41.2 million and adjusted earnings per diluted share were $0.46. The company authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $100 million. For the first quarter of 2026, IPG expects revenue between $235 million and $265 million, adjusted gross margin between 37% and 39%, and adjusted earnings per diluted share between $0.10 and $0.40. Sales increased in Asia by 19%, North America by 23%, and Europe by 6%, with materials processing accounting for 85% of revenue. Management mentioned improved performance due to strategic initiatives and stronger demand, planning for increased revenue growth in key areas like medical and defense. The company focused on operational discipline and innovation to support long-term growth and profitability.

data.title: PodcastOne (PODC) reports $46M revenue for 9 months fiscal 2026, Q3 revenue $15.9M, up 516% in EBITDA. Cash balance up 217% to $3.4M. Guiding $68-75M revenue and $6-10M EBITDA for fiscal 2027.
data.description: PodcastOne (NASDAQ: PODC) announced their Q3 Fiscal 2026 and YTD Fiscal 2026 results, with revenue up 25% YoY to $15.9 million in Q3 and 21% YoY to $46.0 million YTD. Adjusted EBITDA also saw significant growth, surging 516% YoY to $2.8 million in Q3 and 421% YoY to $4.5 million YTD. The company's cash balance increased by 217% YoY to $3.4 million. They added 25 new podcasts, maintained a Top 10 Publisher status, and achieved record revenue from key partnerships. Additionally, they sold three titles to major platforms. Fiscal 2026 guidance includes revenue of $58-$60 million and Adjusted EBITDA of $5-$6 million. PodcastOne remains focused on strategic growth and delivering compelling programming. Financial data for the company shows a positive trend in revenue and EBITDA performance. PodcastOne's conference call and webcast were scheduled for February 12, 2026.

data.title: Grace Therapeutics reported a net loss of $2.3 million in the third quarter, with $18.7 million in cash reserves as of Dec 31, 2025.FDA set a PDUFA target date of April 23, 2026 for GTx-104 review. Company secured $4 million in funding through common warrants. The STRIVE-ON trial demonstrated positive results in aSAH patients treated with GTx-104, showing improved outcomes. Pre-commercial planning continues as the company anticipates potential FDA approval for GTx-104. For more info, visit gracetx.com.
data.description: Grace Therapeutics announced on February 12, 2026, that the FDA has set April 23, 2026, as the PDUFA target date for reviewing the submission seeking approval of GTx-104 for the treatment of aneurysmal Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (aSAH). Results from their Phase 3 STRIVE-ON Safety Trial presented at the 2025 Society of Vascular and Interventional Neurology Annual Meeting showcased improved clinical outcomes with GTx-104. The financial results for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, reported a net loss of about $2.3 million. They secured $4.0 million in funding through common warrant exercises, with cash and cash equivalents at $18.7 million as of December 31, 2025. The STRIVE-ON trial compared GTx-104 with oral nimodipine in aSAH patients, showing a 19% reduction in hypotension incidents in GTx-104 patients. Company's other asset portfolios include GTx-102 and GTx-101 for different medical conditions. The Company believes its current cash will sustain operations for at least 12 months post the press release date. Risk factors and uncertainties can affect the Company's future prospects and outcomes. For further details, visit www.gracetx.com.

data.title: LiveOne (Nasdaq: LVO) reports $58.2M revenue in 9-mo fiscal 2026; audio division earns $52.2M revenue, $3.7M+ EBITDA*. Market-friendly initiatives include reduced expenses by 52%, share repurchases worth $6M left. Expanding partners, AI boosts ARPU by 60%, Premium conversions by 22%+. CEO positive about Q3 results. ADJ EBITDA* increased by $0.1M yr-on-yr. LiveOne focuses on music, entertainment, and technology platforms.
data.description: LiveOne, a music, entertainment, and technology platform, reported a 52% reduction in quarterly operating expenses year-over-year, leading to a decrease in staff from 350 to 88 employees. The company also acquired 771K shares of PodcastOne during Fiscal 2026. Quarterly revenue for Q3 Fiscal 2026 was $20.3M, with Adjusted EBITDA of $1.6M. Audio Division revenue for the same quarter was $18.6M with an Adjusted EBITDA of $2.6M. The company expanded its share repurchase program and announced Fiscal 2027 revenue guidance of $85-$95M+ with Adjusted EBITDA of $8-10M+. LiveOne is actively evaluating M&A opportunities and focusing on AI-driven efficiencies to enhance revenue and profitability.

data.title: Align Partners Capital Management Inc. proposes changes to Dentium Co., Ltd.'s governance and capital allocation at upcoming annual meeting.
data.description: In South Korea, Align Partners Capital Management Inc., a shareholder of Dentium Co., Ltd., has submitted shareholder proposals for Dentium’s 26th Annual General Meeting. The proposals aim to enhance governance practices and optimize capital allocation. Align Partners emphasizes the importance of these actions as they will be presented at the upcoming AGM.

data.title: LHV Group held investor webinar on 12 Feb presenting 2026 financial plan and 5-year forecast; led by Mihkel Torim and Meelis Paakspuu.
data.description: LHV Group recently conducted an investor webinar on 12 February to unveil their financial plan for 2026. During the webinar, Mihkel Torim, Chairman of the Management Board, and Meelis Paakspuu, Chief Financial Officer, presented a comprehensive overview of the five-year financial forecast and strategic objectives. The webinar provided insights into the company's future plans, highlighting key figures and strategies to potential investors and stakeholders.

data.title: AMC Networks seeks consent to amend notes due 2032 to allow up to $50M in equity acquisitions and revise trademark transfer limits.
data.description: On February 12, 2026, AMC Networks Inc. (Nasdaq: AMCX) revealed plans to seek Consents from holders of its 10.50% Senior Secured Notes due 2032. The objective is to modify the Notes' indenture by allowing buybacks, acquisitions, or redemptions of the company's equity interests up to $50 million. Additionally, proposed changes include limiting transfers of specific trademarks to non-exclusive licenses only and restricting investments in unrestricted subsidiaries to defined clauses within the "Permitted Investments" category.

data.title: Cronos Group Inc. will discuss 2025 Q4 and full-year earnings on Feb. 26, 2026, 8:30 am ET.
data.description: Cronos Group Inc. will be hosting their Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings conference call on February 26, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET. This event will provide insights into the company's financial performance and will include a Q&A session with Cronos' senior management. Investors and analysts can participate in the call to learn more about Cronos’ financial results and pose questions to the management team. This conference call is a significant event for shareholders and those interested in the company's performance.

data.title: Midland Exploration Inc. outlines 2026 exploration plans for Gold and Copper-Gold projects in Quebec with partners.
data.description: In Montreal on February 12, 2026, Midland Exploration Inc. provided a report on its gold and copper-gold exploration endeavors for 2026 in Quebec. The company, listed as Midland (TSX-V: MD), highlighted its focus on multiple projects in collaboration within the Abitibi belt, Eeyou Istchee James Bay region, and Labrador Trough. The objective of these exploration activities is to progress numerous gold and copper projects within Midland’s project collection, with a strategic vision towards advancing their resource potential in undisclosed regions of Quebec.

data.title: North American Niobium advances Seigneurie Project in Québec by submitting ATI application for diamond drilling, Feb 12, 2026.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on February 12, 2026, North American Niobium and Critical Minerals Corp. (CSE: NIOB) (FSE: KS82.F) (OTCQB: NIOMF) has given an update on its Seigneurie Project in Québec. The Project, situated in Longue-Rive, Québec and fully-owned by the Company, is currently undergoing the permitting process. North American Niobium has filed its initial application for an Authorization for Impact-Causing Exploration Work (ATI) with Québec’s Ministère des Ressources naturelles et des Forêts (MRNF), the governing body for the ATI process in the province. The ATI is a necessity in Québec for commencing certain exploration activities that could cause impact, such as diamond drilling.

data.title: BridgeBio's Phase 3 trial of Oral Infigratinib in Achondroplasia shows significant body proportionality improvement, hitting primary endpoint (p<0.0001) - AHV at Week 52.
data.description: The PROPEL 3 study achieved success by meeting the primary goal of demonstrating a significant change in AHV compared to baseline at Week 52, with a p-value of less than 0.0001.
